svg.text: How VCF data is stored in GraphMana's graph database
Node types, property layout, and the positional linkage between Variant and Sample nodes
Input: one line of a multi-sample VCF
chromosome → Chromosome node · variant fields → Variant node · sample columns → Sample nodes via positional packed_index
Graph model: node types, properties, and typed edges
Per-sample packed byte arrays (grow with N)
1 bit/sample when present, e.g. on chrX / chrY / mtDNA
↑ sample slot i lives at bit offset 2·(i mod 4) in byte ⌊i/4⌋ — no edge, positional lookup via Sample.packed_index ↑
(ac, an, af, het_count, hom_alt_count, het_exp — one entry per population)
an[] uses called_packed as the denominator:
an[EUR] = 2 × Σ(called_packed[i]=1 for i in EUR)
Array length = K (number of populations) · constant in N · serves TreeMix, SFS, allele-frequency queries
qual, filter, ancestral_allele, is_polarized, multiallelic_site, allele_index, liftover_status
Typed edges out of this Variant node:
→ :Gene via HAS_CONSEQUENCE {type, impact, transcript_id, annotation_source, annotation_version}
No (:Sample)-[:CARRIES]->(:Variant) edge. Sample–variant linkage is positional, via Sample.packed_index → gt_packed offset.

g.text: geneId, symbol, chr, start, end, strand, biotype
(:HAS_CONSEQUENCE edges carry annotation_source + annotation_version for multi-version annotation layers)

svg.text: How GraphMana answers: "what is HG00097's genotype at variant rs587697622?"
No edge traversal at any step. Sample lookup and Variant lookup are O(log n) in their own indexes; steps 3–5 are O(1) arithmetic.

Phase is read the same way from phase_packed (1 bit); called_packed gates whether the sample was actually interrogated. If called_packed[1]=0, the query returns "not looked at" regardless of gt_packed.
Four features of GraphMana's graph model
Samples and Variants are not joined by edges
gt_packed byte array indexed by that
integer. The link is positional, computed
by bit-shift arithmetic at query time.
